QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_67

question.text: When persons acting in concert together hold 15% or more of the open interest on a particular underlying index, they must:

answer.text: Report this fact to the Exchange/Clearing Corporation; failure to do so is a violation attracting penal and disciplinary action
Immediately reduce their combined position to below 15% within one trading day
Obtain prior approval from SEBI before building such a position
No reporting requirement exists; index derivatives have no position limits

explanation.text: **The Logic:** If persons acting in concert hold ≥ 15% of OI on an index, they must report to the Exchange/CC. This is a disclosure requirement, not a prohibition. Failure to report is a violation under exchange rules. **The Trap:** The 15% triggers a REPORTING requirement, not a position reduction mandate. They can hold the position but must disclose. **Cross-Exam Context:** Reporting requirements are regulatory obligations tested in Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 15% OI on index = mandatory reporting. Not a limit per se, but failure to report = violation.
